title: angelica matsumurae
intro: Angelica matsumurae, also known as Omika in Japan, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It is traditionally used in East Asian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating menstrual cramps, and improving digestion. Angelica matsumurae is typically prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine and Japanese kampo, often in combination with other herbs.
